review_text: Fantastic place and pet friendly staff fantastic i would highly recommend going here

Edited: 1 star to bring attention to guests with dogs who wash pet bedding in the washing machines — please be respectful to other guests. It’s about safety and hygiene.

The park itself is great and very well cared for, I will rate it 4 ⭐ However, the laundry facilities need more attention.

Around 90% of the guests had pets, often more than one dog, and many times the dryer filters were completely full of dog fur. There is a notice asking guests not to wash pet bedding, but unfortunately it seems to be ignored.

The park should consider having one washing machine and dryer dedicated to pet items and enforce the rules more strictly for the comfort and hygiene of all guests.

Please follow the park rules and have fun! Thanks.

Definitely worth a visit. Arrived in our caravan. Sites were big, staff accommodating and facilities were great. Had a lovely 2 days, swam in the pool, walked the beach and had fish and chips at the local restaurant. Also not far from Emu Park worth a visit.
Great site, great swimming in the pools, excellent for our pooch, all staff were friendly & helpful. The decorations in the ladies toilets were very cute. All facilities were very clean. Cheers

Was the best experience for our little family especially Easter time they had an Easter Bunny going around the park the kids loved it. I would 100% recommended this park as a couple or as a family. The kids loved the jumping pillow, pool and not far to the beach also the icecream man come as well

Lovely campsite with plenty to do for the kids. The staff were friendly and very helpful. It's a lovely location within walking distance from the beach and close to plenty of shops, cafes and restaurants. We stayed on a powered site with our swags and gazebo and had plently of room.

We stayed over the Easter weekend and the outdoor movies with popcorn were great!!

Beautiful park and bathrooms/camp kitchen clean and convenient. Only negative part was the residents who live there full time was rude to the kids (understand no one should walk through there). Awesome location and close to emu park.
Great place.

Spotless amenities,a credit to the cleaners,very nice staff,crystal clean pools,numerous cooking areas,close to yepoon, very well maintained,I usually only stay 5 days in a park,but I think I might just stay another 5,I'd highly recommend this park

Huge shout out to Casey and Kim the "Amenities legends ". Amenities always so spotless it seems a pity to use them. When i say " spotless " i mean almost up to standard for heart surgery !!!  Thanks ladies. Mike 92
Was on beach front. Clean rooms. Location was perfect. Very private.
Couldn't ask any better will definitely go back excellent staff very clean value for money highly recommended

We had a powered site , park is clean tidy , well set out .
Amenities are really  good and clean
Definitely recommend staying here
The place was a great place to be at overnight. Only issue is that the floors in our room were filthy, it was quite obvious that they weren't mopped. We couldn't let our baby crawl around on it.
Beautiful beach in front of the campground. Amenities very good and two small pools. The site is good
Some of the best bathroom facilities I've seen at a camper park. Beautiful spot and every staff we met we very welcoming and friendly.
Unable to comment on the rooms as we were towing a caravan.

We stayed in the brand new four-bedroom house at the caravan park and it was absolutely beautiful. The house was wonderfully furnished and felt modern, clean, and comfortable — perfect for our family.

The kids loved the swimming pool and having the freedom to ride their scooters around the park. While the games in the games room weren’t working, the pool table was available for a small cost, which was still a nice option.

The location is excellent — close to Emu Park, Yeppoon, and the ferry to Great Keppel Island, making it easy to explore the area.

We did experience an issue with the new driveway leading to the cabin area. The freshly laid bitumen caused rocks and tar to stick to our shoes, which were then tracked onto the verandas and into our car. Aside from this, everything else was great.

Overall, it was a wonderful stay and helped us create special core memories for our children over Christmas. We would happily return.

Well set out and tidy caravan park.
Short walk to beach.
Small drive to Emu Park or Yeppon.
Dog friendly.

This Xmas as my partner and I were meeting up with family and friends  for the first time and some along time since we'had spent Xmas together. When we had realised that Booking.com had just taken our money and booked us in for January 2026 we thought our holiday was over before it even begun.  I was that ardiment it wasn't going to happen, I started to unpack the car.  Fortunately Cath at reception had seen that I had  emailed her explaining that Booking.com had made the wrong booking for us and  went above and beyond for us to get a refund and then booked a cabin the for us straight away. The staff are genuinely friendly and helpful, the amenities are spotless and when we arrived our cabin was already cooled and very tidy. Thanks to Cath you saved our Xmas mate love your work.

This is a lovely campsite with good sized pitches, loads of interesting vegetation, fantastically powerful showers, a nice swimming pool, friendly, helpful staff and easy access to the beach. As we like to park out camper and walk to nearby towns the location was not ideal as to get to Emu Park involved walking along a 80kph road with no footpath, but in a vehicle both Yeppoon and Emu Park are easily accessible.
There was good wifi at the hotspot outside the main office, but very little at the pitch.
Overall a very comfortable, pleasant campsite.
Beau bungalow en bord de route. La plage est situé juste en face.  Très bon accueil et superbe piscine !
Preis Leistung ist gut, schöner angenehmer pool , Toiletten und shower waren in Ordnung
Although we did not manage to get an ensuite unit , it turned out to be great experience .||The common rest room and bath room are clean .||Staff service was good .||We enjoyed our stay
Great staff clean rooms more than happy to help

Just here for the night and great location across from the beach. The lady at reception was very kind and helpful and got us checked in quick and easy.

Very clean well kept park with great facilities. Big bathrooms for shower and WC, nice flat grill BBQs, pool was so clean and refreshing. They have a beautiful firepit area with surrounding chairs.

They have a dump station, laundr, play area for the kids.

Would definitely stay here again. No wifi as got taken down with the storm a s reception is very weak.

A fantastic park just minutes to Yeppoon and the gateway to the Great Keppel Island group. Comes complete with well maintained, modern facilities, manicured grounds and a staff group who are committed to ensuring you have an enjoyable stay. While the beach immediately across the road won’t likely ring your bell too much with its excessive low tide mark and mud flats, the park’s swimming pool area is a standout and well and truly makes up for the beach’s shortfalls.

Great place! The staff is awesome, close to the sea. I recommend it.
Well done!

Secretly i don’t want to tell about how lovely it was at this park. From check in to check out nothing seemed to be too hard for all staff to help. It was our first trip and I will hold it as my standard expectation. Was beautiful & relaxing & very clean. Definitely will be back!
We had a wedding party use the units to get ready for the big day . Cathy and her staff went above and beyond to help them it was a great morning. Thank you ☺️
What a lovely park, kinka beach is just beautiful 😍

We booked large slab caravan sites and we had a great stay. All the amenities are super clean, pool area is great, all the staff are lovely, right across from the beach, quiet and peaceful, definitely will stay here again! Thanks for having us!
Great campsite. Clean amenities, camp kitchen and pools. Opposite the beach. The perfect campsite for exploring the Great Keppel Island as the port is close by. Really enjoyed our stay.

Paid over $70 a night for a powered site..sight unseen thinking Ingenia Park would be really nice for that price amenities out dated the tiles in the showers need regrouting very sharp on you feet they are not locked and birds flying in and out and pooping everywhere the beach across the road is nothing to rave about the pools are small and a blow up jumping pillow not much for the kids to do overall very disappointed for the price give it a miss

Good to know there are places where you can have your dog inside a cabin. Close to the beach, beautiful location.
One thing that annoyed me was watching people throwing their cigarette butts on the ground instead of putting them in the provided bins.

Booked this little gem by chance and love it.  Surrounded by nature, across the road from the beach, nearby shops, amenities spotless and updated.  Waterfall shower heads with great pressure.
Great for the kids as there are 2 pools plus baby pool plus jumping pillow.
Very courteous friendlyand accommodating staff.
I would highly recommend this place for all ages.
Very friendly people and dog friendly .
Amazing location, great staff, awesome amenities, pet friendly. I will definitely come back to stay
Great little caravan park, clean amenities, large sites, across from beach
Hot water ran out in showers but good clean facilities across the road from beach
We stayed here for 4 nights under the new management while we were up here visiting family, what a great place tucked away we thoroughly enjoyed it, thanks to everyone
Excellent spot Great dog walking beach across the road. Clean and convenient facilities

This park is a gem and it runs smoothly, even under the new management.
The coffee shack did not open whilst I was there (3 nights) so I would consider that a step in the wrong direction.
Staff are still friendly and welcoming as was the family before them.
Great place to chill.
Lovely Quiet campsite, in the quiet season it’s like a retirement home - not a kiddy in sight
Love the park, pet friendly and people friendly. Last of the family owned parks in the area. Unfortunately just sold to Ingenia from tomorrow.
Perfectly located, plenty of open space, friendly staff. One of the cleanest parks we stayed in and highly recommend. We will definitely book this park again for a longer stay.
What an awesome caravan park. Loved the adults only areas. The only thing missing was the camp fire.
Beautiful spot to unwind
Great caravan park directly across the road from a great beach.
Wonderful park  great location amenities  are excellent spotless staff friendly and helpful grounds are well kept just a great place

Would have given this a 5 if the laundry had been functional. The washers were fine @$6 a load but of the three dryers, only one worked and at $6 for 25 miserable minutes, at least three sessions were required to get the clothing anywhere near dry. Not good enough. |On the PLUS side, the facilities were great, sites excellent, location awesome and reception really friendly. It was a quiet camp and even though we were very close to the road, there was little noise from it and not enough to be noticeable. Thoroughly recommend
I had the most peaceful quiet stay. Facilities very clean. Family owned with so much pride and love. Crystal was amazing. Will definitely be back. Thank you Island View ❤️

We stayed on an ensuite site with our van.  Great shower hot water and pressure very good, very quiet safe site,  there was a Thai food van outside one night food was good.   Bit of a drive to Yeppoon but we did enjoy visiting Emu Park we were there for the dawn Anzac Day event.
Great caravan park. Nice location opposite beach. Nice clean amenities. Great staff.

Heel mooie locatie vlakbij het strand. Schoon en verzorgd. Wifi alleen in de recreatie ruimte.
We hadden alleen een plek met stroom.

We stayed in our own van, the location was great, the park was tidy and quiet. The amenities were clean.
There was a Thai food van out front, so yumm and a great convenience.
Overall, this was great and we will definitely recommend and will return.

Terrible People to deal with,
One of the worst Caravan Parks we have ever dealt with. Unfortunately the owners are Not God People which is a shame. They would not give us a credit or some sort of a small refund we did not expect a full refund as we mentioned on the phone & we genuinely understand you have a business to run & we do genuinely respect that however with our unfortunate situation that was totally out of our control we were not able to drive to your park which we were lookingfirward to so we thought you would be empathetic to our terrible situation through xmas. The van & vehicle was towed 800kms back home where we had to repair our vehicle but by the grace of God we arrived home safe. Our genuine intention was to come to your park as we had paid approx 6 months in advance. We just could Not believe how rude the owners Who are a husband & wife team which is lovely that you work together and form a strong bond, its great to see that, its just a shame when we asked for a possible credit of some sort or even some sort of refund due to our unfortunate circumstances tou just abruptly told us no which was quite hurtful & we even let you know days before that we were having car problems even then it seemed you did Not Care. It is Not about the refund nor the credit but the fact that you showed us NO EMPATHY, NO CARE OF OUR SITUATION THAT WAS MOST HURTFUL. Please understand we are Honest people & Honest Christians and have always been good people & we always try to do the right thing by God & by the people. We are human beings just please treat us nicely God watches over all of us & we believe in doing the right thing. We hope that you start to do the right thing by your customers & treat us all with dignity, respect & Honesty. God Bless 🙏🙏🙏

My Boyfriend and I are on a five weeks roadtrip up the eastcoast. So we are almost every day on an new Campground (free and paid).

From the 18.02-19.02 we visit the Island View Caravan Park with our Campervan on a powered site.
It is a very clean and beautiful campground.
We got a site in the sun but the owner (who was working on something next to us) asked immediately, if we want a site in the shade(nr.15). We agreed :) very considerate!

We stayed the rest of the day at the two pools. You can lay down on a lounger in the sun or in the shadow. Perfect!

The amenities are very clean, in the girls bathroom are two hairdryers. On every toiletdoor are cute little pictures. You can feel that someone is taking very good care of the place and putting some thought into it.

We cooked our dinner at the campkitchen. You got the bbq station and one gasstove. The kitchen is great and clean.

A few mosquitoes are around but you can handle with that (normal with a lake near by).
The campground is very quiet and we slept till 8am (that never happend before).

Awesome family run caravan park with mini resort feel just across the road from the beach.
Been travelling for 6mths & this was the nicest caravan park we've stayed in yet.
Dog friendly. Lovely staff all round with 'can do' attitude. Clean amenities with lovely little touches like girly bathrooms & washing baskets in laundry. Daily rubbish service. Lovely pool area. Great caravan spots.
Close enough to Yeppoon, not to be in the hustle & bustle. It really is a lovely place to spend a week or three!
And no, I didn't get paid or a free night to do this review. What I got was a really enjoyable stay at a really nice spot on the map 🙏💜
Very tightly fitted in when van park is full. Neighbours that where permanent there Dog barked alot

A Horrible Place to visit I stayed there a little while ago with my sister. The Staff were not very nice to deal with VERY RUDE AND LIED TO US QUITE A FEW TIMES. I SEE THEY HAVE SOME BAD REVIEWS ON HERE MAYBE IT'S TIME YOU STOPPED TREATING PEOPLE SO RUDELY & TREAT PEOPLE WITH RESPECT. I WILL NOT RECOMMEND YOUR CARAVAN PARK TO ANYONE. GOODBYE & GOOD RIDDENS.
On a caravan site so cant comment on rooms . The sites , staff , location and cleanliness the whole park is and always has been fantastic , well worth the stay

We enjoyed our week at Kinka Beach.  The sites are spacious and grounds well maintained.  The amenities were always spotless, and the gentleman who cleaned the amenities was always friendly, as was the office staff. The pools are a great size for everyone.  I think once upon a time, you may have had island or beach views, but due to the overgrowth of bushes, etc, across the road, there is no view sadly.   The caravan park and beach are dog friendly, with off leash allowed before 8am and after 4pm. There is an  access point to the beach opposite the office.   The caravan park is approximately 10 minutes from Emu Park and Yeppoon.
Clean, nice pool , close to beach but a bit far from shops

This is a lovely family owned, pet friendly caravan park situated across the road from Shoal Bay and only a few minutes' drive to Yeepoon and Emu Park. We booked for 2 nights and got 1 free, so end up being $ 50 per night for 2 adults, 2 kids, and dogs, which currently is pretty good. Staff were all friendly, and the grounds, toilets, and pool areas were well maintained.
Very rude staff.  Avoid at all costs.

Not a great stay here unfortunately. big 4 "affliate" park. cabin was ok, a bit older but clean enough. don't think the toilet/shower block had been cleaned all week.
Pools were nicely presented, but freezing, car covered in bat droppings and peacock dropings as they nest in the trees.
Quiet little town but not one we will be venturing back to.

Great caravan park with good amenities and all the different animals roaming around. Close to beach to walk the dogs. Only let down was our site was extremely narrow, only about 500mm wider than our van with awning out.

We are caravanners of many years experience this is one of the most friendly parks we have stayed in for many years most parks  now are owned by multi nationals ,banks ,super funds or cooperate bodies total loss of personal touch manager runs the park under control of head office all about $ & % NOT THIS PARK.  They run bingo every second Friday local traders donate prizes  all raffles profits go to charities  the park has donated over$400000 in the 11 years they have been in business, Well Done top JOB.

Clique'

Wouldn't go back, plenty of others to choose from

Nice little park right across from the beach. Amenities were well kept, clean and great showers.
Nice pool and BBQ area. We loved the chooks and peacocks roaming the park.
Terrific place, well run, friendly staff, great amenities & it's dog friendly. We will return!!
Lots of trees and grass areas. Great facilities. Well maintained

What a lovely caravan park. The amenities are excellent and kept very clean. It is very well managed and the staff are very friendly and helpful.|You are only a few minutes from the lovely Kinka Beach which stretches for over 3 km.|Well worth staying here.

Excellent location and wonderful, helpful staff.
Thanks and we will be back.

Excellent service and friendly
Beautiful beach.
Clean facilities.

What a great find! A beautifully curated caravan park in a bush setting. Lush gardens give shade hold so many facilities.
Adults only pool and area, kids pool, playground, camp kitchens and laundries.

My favourite was the private bathrooms. Lock the door and you have your own shower, toilet, vanity and space to get ready for the day. Great for families and grey nomads alike.

Lovely owners, super helpful and a great location with the beach across the road.

Great van park,  very clean,  lovely location,  great management.
One downside is they allow fire pits🔥  which for us was difficult as we are Asthmatic & allergies. The smoke covered most of the park each night.
Otherwise a great van park.
Great spot large area very friendly staff and very PF,will be back

The friendliest and nicest van park we have been in for a long time.
Amenities, including laundry, very clean and tidy.
Dog friendly park and all dogs, and owners, were super friendly and well behaved, the dogs that is, lol. Bindi loved it as much as us
Thank you staff and management, hope to be back some time.

Wanted to book a cabin for 1 night for 1 person and the only one left was a family ensuite and $170 per night, got told it was minimum of 2 but wasn't the receptionist wasnt sure on that. Then they rang back next day saying it's a minimum of 3 nights because they'd "lose out" a week due to beef week. Money hungry greedy people. I knew there was a reason i never wanted to stop in at this place. Too bad they care only about money and not people. Uhwell i saved $510 by telling them to forget it.

The park amenities, garden/lawn are clean & well maintained. The adult pool is a plus.The reception ladies are friendly and helpful. The park is relatively quite and sites are generous in size. The location is excellent between yeppoon & emu park. I highly recommend this park.

The name is an inspiration to walk to the beach where you definitely get the Island View. Less than 100 m to the beach, nice pool but the water is too hot to cool you down, good park layout to pick up ocean breezes, well grassed and treed. At $48 p/n for 2 for a power and water site  it’s fair but the gas refill and washing machines are pricey..
3rd year running this year. Great atmosphere, great staff and great location thats not too far from touristy Yeppoon. Beach right across the road. Perfect.

We came from sunshine coast not knowing what to expect.. but safe to say we have LOVED our 7nights here over the Christmas period it's been busy which is expected but it's not a hectic noisy busy. Staff are extremely friendly and down to earth!
🐶🐱🐁🦜Pet Friendly (just some we have seen while here)
🚻🚽🚿 Super clean were we camped was like your own ensuite but went to the others and they were emaculate ever time👌
🏊 Two pools one for kids and one adults only 🙌.
🚜 Extremely maintained - lawns, property even the access accross the road to the beach.
🏖️ Beach access is directly across the road and beautiful on high tide.
🚘 Only a short drive to both Yeppoon and Emu Park both amazing towns.
☕ Little coffee shake by the pools sells the most amazing coffee and the BEST lemon 🍋 muffins oh and can't forget their nice fat toastie 🤤..
Rubbish is collected like the binman at home 🏡👍.
I hope we make it back here it's absolutely amazing 💯👌.. would have posted pics but wasn't sure how can find on our Instagram page @traveling_with_thelma
Great

Booked in for 6 nights, ended up staying for 30.
Great park, 2 pools, games room, camp kitchen, outdoor bbq area.
Excellent customer service from the owners.
Highly recommended 😎

We bought our van planning on staying a couple of days stayed 6. Very affordable, wonder ful staff, beautifully kept grounds and bathrooms.
Thank you for a wonderful relaxing holiday and the book swaps :)
Niki and Pat
This place is a little gem. Perfectly located, quiet, very well priced and the staff are just lovely people. Highly recommend and yes we will be coming back.

5 years of visits
Booked for '24
A beautiful shady park, with grassy sites. Clean amenities. Walk straight across the road to the beach. Handy to Yeppoon and Emu Park.

We absolutely loved this Park so much that we extended our stay twice!
The Sites are easy to manoeuvre into & the streets also seemed wider than most parks we’ve been to so far.
They had a Thai Food Van & Seafood Van out the front on different days. The Thai food was the best we’d ever had (apart from Thailand of course).
They have 2 Pools, one is for adults only.
They provide lined bins to every site & a coaxial cable for the TV reception (not that we needed it).
They have on onsite Coffee Kiosk that also sells baked goods (the lemon & poppy seed muffin was delicious).
There are Beautiful gardens with mature trees that provided shaded areas.
All the staff were extremely friendly.
The park is positioned right across the road from the beach, which was awesome.
It was so hard to leave, but hopefully we can go back there some day.

Had van site 80, can have contained fires on this site. Was well protected from the wind.
Site sizes are on not over wide but deep enough to park your car in front.
Area is clean with good amenities and for those who wish they have onsuite sites.
Great pool area for kids as well as adults.
Reasonable rates in a good location.
Late arvo there are some bats in the area.

This park is beautiful. Lots of shaded areas, pool, games room. Peacocks each afternoon. Bathroom facilities are great.
Only thing missing was a camp kitchen. Extremely disappointed with the lack of kitchen amenities. If you are camping you cannot even boil a jug.
Absolute paradise. Nothing has been forgotten here. There are even hair dryers in the shower block! Shady sites, friendly owners, secluded beaches, this place has it all.
Great caravan Park clean and tidy and great service.
Awesome park Extra great staff.  Clean amenities ( showers a bit small for us larger men ) but overall great place to stay
Very friendly staff and lovely location

Location is beautiful. Amenities clean and adequate. However there is no sullage on the sites,  No TV reception.Very poor phone service and minimal WIFI.We booked 8 months out and requested sites close to our travelling companions however found ourselves at opposite ends of the park. Site 21 is very small and difficult to manoeuvre onto. really only suitable for small motor homes.We could not fold out our awning and had to park on the slab with the van.Barely enough room to set up a chair . We were very disappointed. Our allocated site was certainly very poor value for the money we paid.

Was ok. I wouldn’t say best for kids. No wifi that worked or phone service. Beach was great for the kids during the day but nothing to do at night due to mozzies so wifi at least would have been helpful. The park area was average despite our welcome pack saying there was a jumping pillow kids we’re excited about that but I’m guessing isn’t there anymore as we couldn’t find it. Cabin was clean and a great size for family.

We booked in February 2023 for our stay at Island View for our 3 week stay in June, we were given the option of 2 sites which we were not that happy with, but had to accept because the park was fully booked, on several occasions I contacted the park asking if other sites had become available, and was told there were no others available.  We had stayed at the park on several occasions over the years and we loved it.  Unfortunately this time was different, we had been allocated site 35, directly opposite the new coffee shop and next to the amenities.  Well the coffee shop opens at 7:30am till 10:00am, and is the meeting place for management and who ever is buying coffee, so at 7:30am every morning we were woken up with noise, (unfortunately not everyone wants to be woken at that time, and no thought for people and what they maybe going through and needed to sleep).  It was also school holidays for part of our stay, there were some unruly children and this was reported to Maddie in reception and was asked to pass onto management, but unfortunately obviously that wasn't done.|Will we return NO!

Across from kinka beach (short walk through the dunes), fairly full when we were there with long term and 1-2 day stoppers. The park is very clean, well landscaped and maintained,  great pool, coffee shop, amenities. You can tell these guys care...I also used the wifi in the common room and that was excellent and got me out of a tight spot. Thanks

Park is just perfect. We booked the week stay having no idea what to expect, powered site is the best we have had in our travels so far, large double slab across the road from the beach, backed our 21.6’ van in easily. Amenities are great, staff are very friendly. Pets are welcome. Park is very well located out of the hustle and bustle but close enough to everything to not feel too remote. Satellite TV cable is provided for perfect reception. We will be staying again for sure.

We recently stayed at Kinka Beach Island View Caravan Park which we were looking forward to, unfortunately our experience was a little less than average.  The park itself is nice however the location was out of the way and our Caravan site was so small our car had to be park partially on the road as there was no place to put it and very very little room around our van.  The site was so small the back of van was half a metre from the side of another van just to get the drawbar off the road.  We were told by other regulars that the site used to be one site and was cut in two to make another site.  To make it worse there were bigger sites left vacant the duration of our stay which would have been better suited to our size caravan.  We did ask for a slab site but we were not told and had no idea some grass sites actually had matting down which would have been great if we had been informed, the person we booked through should have been more helpful in accomodating our needs but I guess they don’t need to be as they are fully booked till November so it was take what you get attitude!  If you like being crammed in like sardines then site 86a is for you otherwise I would recommend asking for another site.  I explained this to a staff member when I left along with our initial contact experience when booking being less then average customer service, she apologised but our stay there was disappointing.

Excellent van park. Modern amenities, lovely pool, very nice camp kitchen. Extremely helpful staff. This is the fifth van park we have tried in and around Yappoon, this is the best by far. Huge sites, not crammed in like so many others. First class.
